hero_title: Secure Infrastructure for Digital Asset Supervision
hero_subtitle: SFTY Government supports regulated digital asset oversight with phased deployment, sovereign data control, audit-grade logging, and role-based access tailored for public sector security requirements.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_description: Government technology projects face delays when deployment models lack clear controls over data sovereignty, access governance and audit evidence. Without a procurement-safe design, authorities struggle to meet institutional requirements for secure, phased and auditable digital asset infrastructure.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY provides a secure supervisory control layer with role-based access, evidence-grade audit logs, and phased implementation options designed to meet government procurement and data sovereignty requirements.
workflow_title: From access policy to audit log control
workflow_description: SFTY structures role-based access through permissions, audit logs, evidence retention and governance to support secure, phased government deployment and operational control.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. Supervisory control plane
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides regulators a secure, connected supervisory layer that links licensing, entity, wallet, case, and reporting data for controlled government deployment.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators with secure, procurement-safe infrastructure featuring evidence-grade logging, role-based access, and controlled phased implementation for government environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support secure sovereign deployment for regulators
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how phased implementation reduces procurement adoption risk
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports regulatory audit readiness

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for government procurement?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a procurement-safe supervisory platform designed for regulators, combining secure architecture, access governance, phased rollout and evidence-grade logging to reduce institutional risk during adoption.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support secure deployment?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports controlled deployment via role-based access, data segregation, audit logging and flexible on-premise or private cloud options tailored to government security requirements.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit logs and evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves comprehensive audit logs, case history and evidence-grade records to support review readiness and maintain institutional accountability in regulated digital asset supervision.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects with government registries, supervisory workflows and existing compliance systems using modular integration while respecting data sovereignty and deployment controls.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing supervisory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates as a supervisory layer that connects and structures provider outputs without replacing existing analytics or compliance tools used by government authorities.
final_title: Review secure deployment and procurement design
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports phased rollout, role-based access, audit logs and procurement-safe architecture for government adoption.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
